description: Quality-gate contract for /tl-telar:orchestrate. Phase 2 VALIDATE reads enforcement.* commands and runs each. Strict flags determine whether a non-zero exit blocks COMMIT.

review.description: Risk-tiered per-WU adversarial review policy for Phase 3. The roster SIZE scales with each WU's risk_tier — rigor lives in the plan (Katman 0) and mechanical lenses live in CI (Katman 1), so implementation review stays thin. See scripts/tl-telar-reviewer-roster.js and skills/orchestration/adversarial-code-review.md.

tiers.trivial: Code reviewer only. Security added ONLY if a sensitive path is in scope (the floor). For copy/config/test-only/style changes with no logic surface.
tiers.standard: Code + Maintainability + Security(sensitive-path floor OR high-stakes backend/rust/desktop domain) + BackendCorrectness. UI a11y/perf/ux are covered by CI lenses (a11y_command/perf_command + lint), NOT by LLM reviewers. The default.
tiers.critical: Full roster (Code + Maintainability + Security per domain + BackendCorrectness + FrontendUX + Accessibility + Performance) PLUS qualitative escalations owned by the skills: design-review-gate up front, mandatory negative/adversarial tests, CI flipped strict, forced checkpoint:true (human four-eyes), and Security is NOT sticky across retries.

review.sensitive_path_floor: auth · oauth · authz · session · login · token · jwt · password · secret · credential · crypto · payment · billing · migration · .sql · rls · acl · access-control — matched camelCase-aware. Any in-scope match FORCES a Security reviewer on EVERY tier (never droppable). Encoded as SENSITIVE_RE in the roster script.
review.cross_model_second_review: Independent (Codex/Gemini) second review is config-gated in external-tools.yaml (cross_model_review.enabled), NOT tier-gated: when enabled it runs on EVERY WU regardless of tier. On retry it re-runs targeted at the fix, not the full diff.

autonomy.description: interactive (default) = the orchestrator may pause mid-cycle at checkpoint:true WUs and at self-reflect. unattended = exactly ONE human gate at plan-ready (Step 5): scope + approved UI ASCII drafts + all secrets/inputs are collected up front, then the WU cycle (Step 6) runs to PR-ready with ZERO pauses. UI/visual sign-off is hoisted into plan-readiness, never mid-cycle. Unattended never skips a decision — it makes it earlier. See agents/orchestrator.md -> 'Autonomy model'.

execution.description: Maximum Work Units the orchestrator runs concurrently (background subagents). A WU is dispatched only when its deps are COMPLETE and its file_scope is disjoint from every running WU. Default 3 (matches Anthropic 3-5 subagent guidance); raise only for many small, disjoint WUs — parallel agent runs cost ~15x chat tokens. See scripts/tl-telar-wu-scheduler.js.

enforcement.coverage_command: echo 'coverage not configured - run /tl-telar:setup-orchestration for framework-aware defaults' && exit 0
enforcement.security_command: echo 'security scan (e.g. semgrep) not configured - run /tl-telar:setup-orchestration for framework-aware defaults' && exit 0
enforcement.description_review_ci: The a11y/perf/security commands are the Katman-1 CI lenses that standard-tier review relies on INSTEAD of spawning LLM a11y/perf/security reviewers. For a `critical` WU the orchestrator flips a11y_strict/perf_strict/security_strict to blocking for that WU's gate (see adversarial-code-review.md). Keep these commands populated (axe, perf-budget, semgrep) or standard-tier UI/security coverage degrades to advisory-only.
enforcement.description: Mechanical gates (coverage, file-scope) are always blocking. Mobile-extended gates (perf, size, a11y, security) are advisory by default; set *_strict: true to make them blocking. See rules/quality-gates.md for the tier doctrine.
